news.title: Oil climbs to near six-week high as conflict threatens key routes
news.summary:
STORY: Oil prices climbed over 4% to a near six-week high on Wednesday.

With Brent crude jumping over $95 a barrel.

:: MarineTraffic

It comes with growing concerns about disruptions to key Middle Eastern supply routes....

That's due to escalating hostilities between the U.S. and Iran and threats to shipping by the Iran-backed Houthi militia in Yemen.

:: U.S. Central Command

The U.S. military said it carried out an 11th consecutive night of attacks on Iran. 

This came shortly after the Kuwaiti army announced its air defenses were intercepting Iranian drones.

It means there's renewed conflict over control of the key Strait of Hormuz.

:: MarineTraffic

While the Houthis have opened a new front in the war. 

The Iranian allies have threatened to target vessels carrying Saudi oil in the Bab el-Mandeb strait and announced a naval blockade of Saudi Arabia.

One analyst said market watchers now have a dual-strait worry.

:: MarineTraffic

Bab el-Mandeb has become more important for Saudi crude exports after the U.S.-Iran ceasefire collapsed earlier this month.

:: File

Three oil tankers loaded with Saudi crude for China and India made U-turns in the Red Sea on Tuesday and made for the Suez Canal instead.

In response to the Houthi threats, Asian refiners are seeking to ship crude oil from Saudi’s Red Sea port of Yanbu through the Suez Canal and around Africa.
